text.title: CoT Techniques Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in advanced CoT with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: 10x cost vs zero-shot CoT, no measurable accuracy lift.
antipattern.root-cause: "ToT is the most advanced" mindset; technique chosen for sophistication not fit.
antipattern.fix: Pick pattern by task shape (rule r1); revert to baseline if lift <3pp.

antipattern.description: Self-consistency without an explicit voting rule.
antipattern.symptom: Ties broken arbitrarily; same input produces different outputs across runs.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer assumed "majority" without saying so; categorical vs numerical mismatch.

antipattern.description: Branches leaked to user — ToT scratch paths shown in the UI.
antipattern.symptom: Confusing UI with rejected paths visible; sometimes contradictory candidates.
antipattern.root-cause: Output not filtered after branch selection.
antipattern.fix: Return only winning branch; log all branches server-side for audit.

antipattern.description: No cost guard — misconfigured N or depth blows the budget.
antipattern.symptom: Single call costs >$0.50; daily spend triples.
antipattern.root-cause: "N=10 is fine" without per-call cost calculation.
antipattern.fix: max_usd_per_call hard cap; abort + log if breached (rule r4).

antipattern.description: No baseline measurement — advanced pattern shipped without zero-shot comparison.
antipattern.symptom: Cannot answer "did this actually help?"; reverting risks regression panic.
antipattern.fix: Measure zero-shot baseline on the same eval set first; only then run A/B (rule r2).
